Git Cheatsheet

CLICK ANY COMMAND TO COPY  ·  AI-AGENT-ASSIST BITBUCKET  ·  INSIGHTS & AUTOIGNITE GITHUB

Repo & User Info — check where you are and who you are
What repo am I connected to? (shows github / bitbucket URL)
$
git remote -v
copy
Which user is configured for commits?
$git config user.name
$git config user.email
copy
What branch am I on and what has changed?
$
git status
copy
New Branch from QA
1. Fetch latest from remote
$
git fetch origin
copy
2. Switch to QA
$
git switch qa
copy
3. Reset local QA to exactly match origin
$
git reset --hard origin/qa
copy
4. Create and switch to new branch
$
git switch -c feature/AI-XXXX
copy
5. Push and track remote branch
$
git push -u origin feature/AI-XXXX
copy
Commit & Push
Check what has changed
$
git status
copy
Stage all changes
$
git add .
copy
Stage a specific file
$
git add path/to/file
copy
Commit with message
$
git commit -m 'feat: your message'
copy
Push current branch
$
git push
copy
Push new branch for the first time
$
git push -u origin HEAD
copy
Restore Files from origin/qa
Restore any specific files
$
git restore --source=origin/qa -- 'file1' 'file2'
copy
Your common config files
$
git restore --source=origin/qa -- 'ui/vite.config.mts' 'ui/components.d.ts'
copy
Discard ALL local changes — careful!
$
git restore .
copy
Branch Management
List all branches (local and remote)
$
git branch -a
copy
Switch to existing branch
$
git switch branch-name
copy
Delete local branch
$
git branch -d feature/AI-XXXX
copy
See last 10 commits
$
git log --oneline -10
copy
Reset & Sync ⚠ these overwrite local changes
Fetch and reset QA to match origin exactly
$git fetch origin
$git switch qa
$git reset --hard origin/qa
copy all
Pull latest on current branch
$
git pull
copy
Stash changes and restore later
$
git stash — save for later
copy
$
git stash pop — restore
copy
Copied to clipboard ✓
